# github-workflow MCP needs systematic boot + wiring + integration test coverage

### Context

PR [#10997](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10997) (M6 github-workflow SDK migration, merged 2026-05-08) introduced TWO broken relative-import paths in `ai/services/github-workflow/toolService.mjs` that survived all 39 cited unit tests:

1. **`ToolService` import** (line 11): stale 3-dot path resolving to repo-root → caught + fixed in PR [#11104](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11104) (#11103) merged 12:57Z 2026-05-10
2. **`openapi.yaml` path** (line 16): stale 1-up path resolving to non-existent `ai/services/openapi.yaml` → caught + fixed in PR [#11106](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11106) (operator-approved piggyback under #11105 scope) currently APPROVED + CLEAN

Two co-resident bugs, same migration, same file. **CI green throughout.** The bugs only surfaced empirically when:
- Operator @tobiu detected `mcp__neo-mjs-github-workflow__*` tools missing post-harness-restart
- I invoked `/self-repair` Phase 1 step 4 (native terminal boot-test) — surfaced bug #1 in <30 seconds
- Operator surfaced bug #2 from his post-merge review of the substrate path-resolution

### The Problem

`test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/` has 6 spec files for individual reusable services (DiscussionService, IssueService, IssueSyncer, LabelService, PullRequestService, SyncService.Stage2). **None test the wiring layer (`toolService.mjs` aggregator) NOR the MCP server boot path.**

Compare to sibling structure: `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/` has `BaseServer.spec.mjs` (23KB) + per-server subdirs (memory-core has 8 spec files). github-workflow has NO subdir under `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/`.

This systematic test gap lets wiring bugs survive PR review:
- **Per-service unit tests** import service classes directly (IssueService, etc.) — they don't exercise the aggregator
- **Static diff review** can miss path-resolution semantics that fail only at module-load time
- **Boot smoke test** (just-running `node mcp-server.mjs`) catches BOOT crashes but misses lazy-load failures (the openapi.yaml bug fell into THIS gap — `ToolService` loads openapi lazily on first `listTools()`/`callTool()`)

@tobiu's M6 4-item retrospective (2026-05-10) framed it directly:
> "if a breaking import path keeps our CI tests green, we are missing tests for gh workflow"

### The Fix

Add 3 layers of test coverage to `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/`:

1. **Boot smoke test** (`Boot.spec.mjs`): spawn `node ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/mcp-server.mjs` as child process, assert it survives a 5-second alive-probe. Catches the `ToolService` import bug class (boot-time module-resolution failures).

2. **Wiring test** (`ToolService.spec.mjs`): import `toolService.mjs`, call `listTools()`, assert the returned list matches the `serviceMapping` keys + the `openapi.yaml` content. Catches the openapi path bug class (lazy-load module-resolution failures + serviceMapping/openapi drift).

3. **Integration test** (`MCPHandshake.spec.mjs`): spawn the MCP server, perform a JSON-RPC handshake via stdio, call a representative `listTools` + `callTool` round-trip. Catches downstream-of-wiring failures (e.g., openapi parse errors, serviceMapping handler binding failures).

Mirror sibling pattern: see `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/` for the convention. Same 3-layer shape would have caught BOTH PR #10997 bugs at PR-review time.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/Boot.spec.mjs` — 5s alive-probe + clean exit on SIGTERM
- [ ] `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/ToolService.spec.mjs` — `listTools()` returns expected tool names; `openapi.yaml` resolution matches `ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/openapi.yaml`
- [ ] `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/MCPHandshake.spec.mjs` — JSON-RPC `tools/list` + at least one `tools/call` round-trip succeeds
- [ ] All 3 specs run as part of `npm run test-unit` (no special harness configuration)
- [ ] Validation: temporarily revert PR #11104's import fix → confirm `Boot.spec.mjs` fails. Temporarily revert PR #11106's openapi fix → confirm `ToolService.spec.mjs` fails. Both bugs would have been caught.

### Out of Scope

- Test coverage for OTHER M6-migrated MCP servers (memory-core, knowledge-base, neural-link) — they have existing test directories + their toolService.mjs files weren't broken in the M6 migration. Consider parallel audit in a separate ticket if needed.
- E2E / whitebox tests (those run via `whitebox-e2e` skill; this ticket is unit-test-substrate scope)
- Refactoring the `ToolService` aggregator pattern (see #11107 for the architectural-move follow-up)
- CI gating policy changes (e.g., requiring all 3 spec files to pass before allowing merge)

### Avoided Traps / Gold Standards Rejected

#### Decision Matrix

1. **3-layer test coverage at `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/` (Selected):** Mirrors the sibling pattern (memory-core has 8 spec files, knowledge-base has its own subdir). Each layer catches a distinct bug class (boot vs lazy-load vs handshake). Lowest-risk addition; uses existing test harness conventions.

2. **Boot smoke test only:** Rejected. Catches the `ToolService` import bug class but misses the openapi lazy-load bug class. PR #11104 demonstrates the gap: my 3s alive-probe was a primitive boot smoke test that passed even though tool calls would fail.

3. **Integration test only (MCP handshake):** Rejected. Comprehensive but slow; high-friction for fast-CI cycles. Layered approach (boot + wiring + integration) lets fast unit-style probes catch the common wiring bugs without paying integration-test latency on every PR.

4. **CI lint for relative-import-path-correctness:** Rejected as substrate-quality CI scope-creep. Real bugs slip through ANY lint; empirical execution is the gold-standard verifier. Linting could be a follow-up but doesn't substitute for these 3 tests.

5. **Add tests at `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/`:** Rejected. The bugs are at the MCP-server-wiring layer, not the SDK-service-class layer. Sibling pattern places wiring tests under `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/<name>/`. Per-service unit tests exist already at the SDK layer; they're not the gap.

- **Trap:** Treating CI-passing as boot-correctness. **Rejection:** PR #10997 had 39 unit tests passing while shipping 2 broken paths. Per-service tests don't exercise wiring; static diff review missed the path-resolution semantics. Empirical boot-test is the gate that closes this gap.

- **Trap:** Adding tests AFTER bugs surface, not before. **Rejection:** True for #11103/#11104/#11106 — bugs already merged + fixed. But the discipline is: **once a bug class is identified, capture it as a test BEFORE the next migration.** Per @tobiu's "if a breaking import path keeps our CI tests green, we are missing tests" framing — this ticket converts that friction to gold for the next M-migration cycle.

### Related

- M6 migration PR #10997 (origin of both bugs)
- PR #11104 (#11103) — first surgical fix (`ToolService` import)
- PR #11106 (#11105) — second surgical fix (openapi.yaml piggyback)
- #11107 — architectural follow-up (move toolService.mjs back to MCP server dirs; obsoletes the surgical fixes)
- AGENTS.md §3.5 V-B-A core value (the empirical boot-test pattern is the V-B-A primitive applied to MCP-server-wiring)
- @tobiu's M6 4-item retrospective (this session, 2026-05-10)
- `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup` MEMORY.md entry (sibling pattern: cross-singleton mutation symmetric resets — same family of test-discipline)
- `feedback_run_related_tests_in_pr_review` (sibling: empirical-checkout substrate-contention rationale; this ticket adds the substrate that makes empirical-checkout meaningful)

> ❋ ## ticket-intake verdict: `needs-narrowing` — scope reduced, then proceeding
> 
> Ran `ticket-intake` (assignee already @neo-opus-4-7; created 2026-05-10, 10 days old, no `stale` label, 0 comments — pre-stale, clean).
> 
> ### V-B-A against current `origin/dev` — two premise claims are now stale
> 
> 1. **"github-workflow has NO subdir under `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/`"** — **stale.** The subdir now exists with two specs: `ConfigCompleteness.spec.mjs` (#10209) and `ToolRegistration.spec.mjs`.
> 2. **The "wiring test (`ToolService.spec.mjs`)" layer** — **now redundant.** @neo-gpt's open PR #11689 (`#11687`) adds `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/McpServerListToolsSmoke.spec.mjs`, a cross-server smoke that — verified — imports each real `toolService.mjs`, asserts `listTools()` names == `openapi.yaml` operationIds == parsed `serviceMapping` keys, per server *including github-workflow*. The existing `ToolRegistration.spec.mjs` overlaps further. The generic listTools/serviceMapping/openapi-drift layer is **doubly covered**.
> 
> The **boot** and **handshake** gaps remain real: `grep -rl "spawn|child_process|handshake|JSON-RPC|mcp-server.mjs"` across the github-workflow test subdir returns **nothing** — no child-process boot probe, no stdio round-trip exists.
> 
> ### Narrowed scope (peer-reconciled with @neo-gpt — `MESSAGE:4373ab22`)
> 
> `ticket-intake` §6.6 source-of-authority collision check was run against PR #11689 (path-orthogonal — #11110 writes only `test/.../github-workflow/*`). Narrowed prescription:
> 
> 1. **`Boot.spec.mjs`** — child-process alive-probe of `ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/mcp-server.mjs` + clean SIGTERM exit. *(Catches the PR #10997 / #11104 import-resolution bug class.)*
> 2. **`MCPHandshake.spec.mjs`** — JSON-RPC `tools/list` + one `tools/call` stdio round-trip (credential-free, with clean skips/fakes if needed).
> 3. ~~`ToolService.spec.mjs` (wiring/drift layer)~~ — **dropped**, covered by #11689 + existing `ToolRegistration.spec.mjs`. Original AC5 (temporary-revert validation of the #11104/#11106 path bugs) folds into the `Boot.spec.mjs` AC as a github-workflow-specific regression assertion.
> 
> Structural-pre-flight: Stage 1 fast-path — the target directory already exists and is populated; the 2 new specs mirror the `memory-core/` sibling shape (`Server.spec.mjs` etc.). No new-directory decision.
> 
> The original ACs 1 + 3 + 5 carry into implementation under the narrowed scope; AC2 (the standalone `ToolService.spec.mjs`) is retired. Proceeding to the branch + implementation lane — this is a peer-confirmed, empirically-grounded scope *reduction* on a self-assigned ticket. The PR body will carry the authoritative narrowed scope.

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-20T19:12:52Z

**Input from Neo Opus 4.7 (Claude Opus 4.7):**

> ❋ ## `[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]` Corrected ticket-intake verdict: `superseded` — supersedes my earlier `needs-narrowing`
> 
> My earlier intake comment returned `needs-narrowing` and narrowed scope to `Boot.spec.mjs` + `MCPHandshake.spec.mjs`. **That verdict rested on an incomplete duplication sweep** — the intake grep was scoped to `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/` only. The implementation-lane sibling study (unit-test skill, "study siblings before writing") swept the broader `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/` tree and found the coverage already exists in the **`client/` sibling tree**. Correcting before any code is written.
> 
> ### The boot + JSON-RPC handshake coverage already exists
> 
> **`test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/client/McpServersHealth.spec.mjs`** (created 2026-04-16, PR #10044 — predates this ticket) loops all five MCP servers **including `github-workflow`** and, per server:
> - `mcpClient.ready()` → boots the server process; `expect(mcpClient.connected).toBe(true)` — the **boot/alive** assertion;
> - `mcpClient.listTools()` → JSON-RPC **`tools/list`**;
> - `mcpClient.tools.healthcheck({})` → JSON-RPC **`tools/call`** stdio round-trip.
> 
> Its test name is literally *"Server '…' should boot, negotiate JSON-RPC, and respond to healthcheck."* That is exactly this ticket's proposed `Boot.spec.mjs` + `MCPHandshake.spec.mjs`, for github-workflow specifically. Writing them would **duplicate `McpServersHealth.spec.mjs`**.
> 
> ### The CI-catchability concern is closed by #11689
> 
> This ticket's load-bearing point is *"a breaking import path kept CI green."* `McpServersHealth.spec.mjs` is a **bucket-D** test (`test.skip(NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI, …)`) — it does not run in credential-less CI, so it alone does not catch import breakage in CI. But:
> - **#11689** (`McpServerListToolsSmoke.spec.mjs` — open, approved, at the merge gate) imports each real `toolService.mjs` **in-process** and asserts `listTools()`; a broken import in `toolService.mjs` fails that smoke, and it is CI-runnable. That closes the CI-catchability gap for the PR #10997 bug class.
> - `ToolRegistration.spec.mjs` + `ConfigCompleteness.spec.mjs` in this subdir are **static text scans** (`fs.readFileSync` + regex on `toolService.mjs`) — they never execute the module, so they neither catch import breakage nor would be duplicated by it.
> 
> ### Verdict
> 
> `superseded` — by `McpServersHealth.spec.mjs` (boot + handshake, pre-existing) + #11689 (`McpServerListToolsSmoke`, the CI-runnable in-process import smoke). The proposed `Boot.spec.mjs` / `MCPHandshake.spec.mjs` would be duplicative test substrate. **Recommended disposition: close `not_planned`** once #11689 merges. Until then `McpServersHealth.spec.mjs` provides the boot+handshake coverage; after, #11689 adds the CI-runnable import smoke. (Per the `ticket-intake` Rejection Protocol, the agent-initiated close of a non-self-created issue is Human-in-the-Loop — presented for operator confirmation.)
> 
> Friction → gold: an intake duplication sweep for a test-coverage ticket must span the whole `test/playwright/unit/ai/<subsystem>/` tree (incl. sibling trees like `client/`), not just the ticket's prescribed target subdir — the coverage lived one tree over. Caught at the implementation-lane sibling-study step, before any duplicative code was written.
> 
> *(Same Substrate Accretion Defense discipline as the #11690 governance arc and the #11307 rejection — applied here to my own backlog.)*
